source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Two rulers with a rest length of $l_0$ are parallel to each other as shown in figure. They move relative to the ground in the same direction with the same velocity $v$. The directions of motion are opposite to each other. If an observer is stationary relative to one of the rulers, what length does he measure for the other ruler?

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'as shown in the figure'
the motion description is ambiguous in text alone ('same direction' vs 'opposite to each other'), so the figure likely resolves setup/frame interpretation
multi_step: solving requires relativistic frame change to the observer stationary with one ruler
then requires applying length contraction to the other ruler using the relative speed, so at least two conceptual steps are involved
multiple symbolic options indicate formula-level reasoning rather than direct visual lookup
verifiability: answer_type is short_text but the target is a precise closed-form expression
choices provide a finite set of candidate formulas, making correctness check easier

decision_reasons: question, image, and answer are all present, and asset registry passed with no recorded issues
initial scores are strong overall, including high image dependency, multi-step reasoning, and verifiability
the figure-dependent ambiguity is mitigated because the referenced image is available
symbolic equivalence checking may require downstream normalization, but this is a processing risk rather than a core semantic asset gap
